title: carlina macrocephala subsp. nebrodensis
intro: Carlina macrocephala subsp. nebrodensis, also known as Sardinian carline thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the island of Sardinia. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Carlina macrocephala subsp. nebrodensis can be found in the form of dried flowers and roots, which are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
